description: Letter in Judaeo-Arabic. Dealing mainly with business matters. The sender is sorting through accounts (e.g., "I checked my ledger (daftarī) and did not find anything."). Mentions people such as Abū Naṣr; Faḍāʾil; Kātib al-Jaysh (who is supposed to provide a letter for Ibn Ṣaghīr); Ibn Hilāl b. Ḥasan; and al-shaykh Maḍmūn. Quantities on the order of 12 dinars are named. The continuation on the back is very faded; the writer mentions marriage several times.
tags: ib-addenda, katib al-jaysh, India Book unedited
description_he: מכתב ביהודית-ערבית. מוחזק בבית המדרש לרבנים.
